rulings.Case Title: JAMES BRADFORD JR. VS. IMO INDUSTRIES, INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Substituting Kelley Bradford As Successor-In-Interest To, And Substituting Successor-In-Interest For, Deceased Plaintiff; For Leave To File First Amended Complaint; And To Add Previously Sued Doe Defendant To The First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 6, 2019 in Department 503, Line 4. Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Substituting Kelley Bradford as Successor-In-Interest to, and Substituting Successor-In-Interest for, Deceased Plaintiff; for Leave to File First amended Complaint; and to Add Previously Sued Doe Defendant to the First Amended Complaint is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: DALAL METWALLY VS. AVON PRODUCTS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction Declaration Of Bartek R Rejch
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 6, 2019 in Department 503, Line 5. Defendant Sherwin-Williams Company's Motion to Quash Service of Summons for Lack of Personal Jurisdiction is continued to October 9, 2019, pursuant to Plaintiff's request to conduct jurisdictional discovery, which is granted. The discovery is limited to written and oral discovery regarding personal jurisdiction as indicated in items 9-10 and 14-26 of Plaintiff's opposition. (Opp at 14-15.) It is further limited to California and the time period between 1970 to 2000. Supplemental opposition, if any, due by September 20, 2019. Supplemental reply, if any, due by September 27, 2019. If a hearing is requested to contest this continuance, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
